chapter_title.en: Chapter on Reports Regarding One Who Digs a Well or Something Else in His Own Property or in another's Property, and a Person Falls Into it and Perishes

text: 5341 - رَوَى زُرْعَةُ وَ عُثْمَانُ بْنُ عِيسَى عَنْ سَمَاعَةَ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَحْفِرَ اَلْبِئْرَ فِي دَارِهِ أَوْ فِي أَرْضِهِ فَقَالَ "أَمَّا مَا حَفَرَ فِي مِلْكِهِ فَلَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ ضَمَانٌ وَ أَمَّا مَا حَفَرَ فِي اَلطَّرِيقِ أَوْ فِي غَيْرِ مِلْكِهِ فَهُوَ ضَامِنٌ لِمَا يَسْقُطُ فِيهَا".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.5341 - Zurah and Uthman ibn Isa narrated from Samaah, who said:
I asked Imam (as) about a man who digs a well in his house or on his land.
Imam (as) said: "If he digs it within his own property, there is no liability upon him. But if he digs it in a public road or in a place that does not belong to him, then he is liable for anything that falls into it."
